title: SQL Injection in login form
evidence: POST /login username=' OR '1'='1 user=analyst@example.com Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9.fake.token
impact: Attackers may bypass authentication or access sensitive records.
recommendation: Use parameterized queries and add regression tests around authentication queries.

title: Missing CSRF protection
evidence: State-changing POST accepted without CSRF token. Cookie: sessionid=fake-session-value; theme=light
impact: An attacker may trick authenticated users into changing account settings.
recommendation: Require CSRF tokens for state-changing requests and validate same-site cookie settings.

title: Weak TLS configuration
evidence: TLS 1.0 accepted from test client phone +1-202-555-0198.
impact: Legacy TLS increases exposure to downgrade and compliance issues.
recommendation: Disable TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1; prefer TLS 1.2 or later with modern ciphers.

title: Directory listing enabled
evidence: Index page exposes sample filenames only.
impact: Directory metadata may reveal application structure.
recommendation: Disable directory indexes and serve only explicitly allowed static files.
